steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical transition to begin the final sentence. The skill is transition words. The decisive clue is the logical relationship: the previous sentences establish that siderophiles are more abundant than the core-differentiation model predicts, and the final sentence offers a causal explanation for that abundance — so the transition must signal a logical consequence or conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "That said,": "That said" signals a concession or qualification, implying the sentence contrasts with or limits the previous information. The final sentence is not a concession — it follows logically from the discrepancy already described. ✗
  • Choice B — "Hence,": "Hence" signals a logical consequence or conclusion drawn from the preceding information. Because siderophiles are unexpectedly abundant, the conclusion that extraterrestrial material must have introduced them follows directly — exactly the cause-and-effect relationship "hence" expresses. ✓
  • Choice C — "For example,": "For example" signals that what follows illustrates the previous claim. The final sentence does not illustrate the abundance of siderophiles — it explains it. ✗
  • Choice D — "Likewise,": "Likewise" signals a parallel or similar point. The final sentence is not presenting a parallel observation; it is providing a causal explanation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
